examples.title: Dynamic Structure Allocation
examples.description: Demonstrates creating, using, and freeing a dynamically allocated structure
examples.explanation: This program shows complete lifecycle of dynamic structure: allocation with malloc(), initialization using arrow operator (->), usage, and proper deallocation with free(). The NULL check prevents crashes from failed allocation.

examples.title: Singly Linked List Operations
examples.description: Implements creation and traversal of a singly linked list using self-referential structures
examples.explanation: Demonstrates self-referential structures for linked lists. createNode() allocates memory and returns pointer. displayList() traverses using temp pointer. countNodes() traverses and counts. Memory is freed in main to prevent leaks.

examples.title: Passing Structure to Function by Reference
examples.description: Shows how to pass structure pointer to function to modify original data
examples.explanation: calculateArea() receives pointer, modifies original using arrow operator. displayRectangle() receives copy (pass by value), changes inside don't affect original. Demonstrates difference between passing value vs reference.

examples.title: Array of Pointers to Structures
examples.description: Demonstrates array where each element is a pointer to a dynamically allocated structure
examples.explanation: library is an array of 3 pointers. Each pointer points to dynamically allocated Book structure. Uses array indexing with arrow operator: library[i]->title. This approach allows flexible memory usage and individual management of each structure.
